---
title: "Applied Data Science Course"
format: "html"
---

## Module 1: Introductions

Learning outcomes:

1. Define Data Science
2. Identify the three circles that make up Data Science
3. Recognize the three roles that a Data Scienist takes on
4. Distinguish between Data Engineers, Data Analysts and Machine Learning Engineers
5. Define Data Analysis
6. Distinguish between CDA and EDA and give one example for each
7. Recognize that outliers can actually inform our investigation and are not to be treated as random noise or errors

Lessons and labs:

1. [Introduction to Data Science Processes and Roles](./M1/lessons/01_slides_intro.qmd)
2. [Lab 1: Confirmatory Data Analysis (CDA)- blind typing experiment](./M1/lessons/02_lab_cda_blind_typing.ipynb) (15m read)
3. [Lab 2: Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)- US Census 2010-2019](./M1/lessons/03_lab_eda_census.ipynb) (30m read)

#### Exercises

1. [John Snow](./M1/exercises/john_snow.qmd) (3 questions)
2. [Review Quiz NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google.com/notebook/8e9ff4ca-2054-48d5-b0d3-120db7d965f8?artifactId=f7631507-8fba-427b-991b-962a13bad964) (10 questions)

## Module 2: Data Wrangling

Learning outcomes:

1. Explain the roles of `DataFrame` and `Series` in pandas and inspect basic metadata (`head`, `tail`, `info`, `dtypes`, `index`, `columns`)
2. Load tabular data into pandas from local files and URLs, and construct a `DataFrame` from Python data structures
3. Select and filter data using column selection, boolean masks, combined conditions, and label/position indexing with `loc` and `iloc`
4. Create and transform columns using vectorized operations, including deriving new columns, dropping columns, and renaming columns
5. Compute descriptive statistics with methods such as `mean`, `median`, `describe`, and `agg`, while interpreting grouped summaries
6. Aggregate data by one or more categories using `groupby` and count category frequencies with `value_counts`
7. Apply custom functions table-wise, row/column-wise, and element-wise using `pipe`, `apply`, and `map`

Lessons and labs:

1. [Lab: Pandas Essentials](./M2/lessons/01_pd_dataframe.ipynb)
2. [Lab: Summary Statistics](./M2/lessons/02_pd_agg.ipynb)
3. [Lab: Reshaping DataFrames)](./M2/lessons/03_pd_reshape.ipynb)
4. [Example: Bike Sharing](./M2/lessons/04_lab_bike_sharing.ipynb)
5. [Lab: Timeseries](./M2/lessons/05_pd_timeseries.ipynb)
6. [Lab: Function application](./M2/lessons/06_pd_function_application.ipynb)

#### Exercises

1. Getting to Know your Data:
    1. [World Food Facts](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/01_Getting_%26_Knowing_Your_Data/World_Food_Facts/Exercises.ipynb)
    2. [Chipotle](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/01_Getting_%26_Knowing_Your_Data/Chipotle/Exercises.ipynb)
    3. [Occupation](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/01_Getting_%26_Knowing_Your_Data/Occupation/Exercises.ipynb)
2. Filtering and Sorting Data
    1. [Chipotle](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/02_Filtering_%26_Sorting/Chipotle/Exercises.ipynb)
    2. [Euro12](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/02_Filtering_%26_Sorting/Euro12/Exercises.ipynb)
    3. [Fictional Army](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/02_Filtering_%26_Sorting/Fictional_Army/Exercise.ipynb)
3. Group By
    1. [Alcohol Consumption](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/03_Grouping/Alcohol_Consumption/Exercise.ipynb)
    2. [Occupation](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/03_Grouping/Occupation/Exercise.ipynb)
    3. [Regiment](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/03_Grouping/Regiment/Exercises.ipynb)

#### Bonus Exercises

4. Apply
    1. [Students Alcohol Consumption](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/04_Apply/Students_Alcohol_Consumption/Exercises.ipynb)
    2. [US_Crime_Rates](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/04_Apply/US_Crime_Rates/Exercises.ipynb)
5. Merge
    1. [Auto_MPG](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/05_Merge/Auto_MPG/Exercises.ipynb)
    2. [Fictitious Names](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/05_Merge/Fictitous_Names/Exercises.ipynb)
    3. [House Market](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/05_Merge/Housing_Market/Exercises.ipynb) 
6. Stats
    1. [US_Baby_Names](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/tree/master/06_Stats/US_Baby_Names/Exercises.ipynb)
    2. [Wind_Stats](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/tree/master/06_Stats/Wind_Stats/Exercises.ipynb)
7. Time Series
    1. [Apple_Stock](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/09_Time_Series/Apple_Stock/Exercises.ipynb)
    2. [Getting_Financial_Data](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/09_Time_Series/Getting_Financial_Data/Exercises.ipynb)
    3. [Investor_Flow_of_Funds_US](https://github.com/HassanAlgoz/pandas_exercises/blob/master/09_Time_Series/Investor_Flow_of_Funds_US/Exercises.ipynb)

#### Videos

If you like walkthroughs, follow this [YouTube Playlist](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLgJhDSE2ZLxaY_DigHeiIDC1cD09rXgJv) which go through the exercises as well.

## Module 3: Statistical Analysis

### M3-1: Univariate Analysis

Learning outcomes:

1. Identify the two types of statistics
2. Distinguish between Population and Sample
3. Given a dataset, identify the numerical and categorical variables
4. Show statistics like: *Proportion*, *Mean* an *Variance* (and calculate them)
5. Identify the two two key elements that describe a Distribution, (and calculate them)
6. Describe different shapes of distributions using terms like: normal/gaussian, uniform, skewed, and exponential
7. Give at least 2 examples of normally distributed data
8. Give at least 1 example of non-normally distributed data (e.g., exponential)
9. Explain what a *Percentile* means in layman terms; (and calculate them).
10. Explain what is 1, 2, and 3 standard deviations mean for a normal distribution (and calculate them)
11. Given a box-and-whiskers plot, identify its graphical elements and tell what they mean
12. Given a plot make general statements about the samples using words like: "most", "few"
13. Given a general statement about a samples, verify whether it is true or not
14. Name at least 2 outlier-removal methods and explain how they work

Lessons:

- [Lesson: Statistics](./M3/lessons/01_slides_statistics.qmd)
- [Lab: Univariate Analysis](./M3/lessons/01_univariate.ipynb)

#### Exercises

1. [Exercise](./M3/exercises/m3-1_exercise.ipynb)

### M3-2: Statistical Visualizations

Learning outcomes:

1. Distinguish between figure and axes in matplotlib
2. Name the three figure-level functions in seaborn
3. Distinguish between Histogram and Barplot
4. Given a DataFrame, make subplots via the `col` and `row` parameters in a figure-level function
5. Given a DataFrame, make subplots and put custom plots using axes-level functions; and have them do `sharex=True` or `sharey=True`
6. Given a dataset, write code to show a box plot
7. Given a dataset, write code to show two distributions on the same figure axes
8. Given a DataFrame, plot the ECDF and interpret it
9. Given a DataFrame, plot a bi-variate histogram and interpret it
10. Visualize numerical vs categorical relationships using `sns.catplot()`
11. Visualize multiple categorical variables in one plot
12. Use different colors, markers, and styles in a plot
13. Relate variables using scatter plot
14. Identify issues in a given visualization and suggest improvements

Lessons and labs:

1. [Data Visualization](./M3/lessons/02_data_viz.ipynb)

#### Exercises

1. [Exercise](./M3/exercises/m3-2_exercise.ipynb)
2. [Visualization Exercises](./M3/exercises/viz_exercises.ipynb)

## Module 4: Inferential Statistics

### M4-1: Hypothesis Testing

Learning outcomes:

1. Distinguish between *Inferential* and *Descriptive* Statistics
2. Distinguish between *Null* and *Alternative* Hypothesis
3. Recognize anecdotal evidence as such
4. Given a statement, define the null and alternative hypothesis to setup the statistical testing procedure
5. Explain what failing to reject the null hypothesis means
6. Given an alternative hypothesis select the correct statistical test
7. Intrepret the result of a statistical test (p-value)
8. Understand that alpha (the significance level) is selected by  convention to have the value of `0.05`
9. Distinguish between *Treatment* and *Control* Groups
10. Given a claim in layman terms, describe it in statistical terms, as a hypothesis
11. Given a hypothesis in statistical terms, explain it in layman terms
12. Recognize when the test is two-sided or one-sided (and in which direction)

Lessons:

- [Lab: Hypothesis Testing](./M3/lessons/03_hypothesis_testing.ipynb)


### M4-2: Bivariate Analysis

Learning outcomes:

1. Distinguish between correlation and association
2. Interpret a give correlation coefficient $r$
3. Given a correlation coefficient $r$ map it to a scatter plot
4. Use `sns.pairplot(kind="reg")` to plot pairwise relationships in a datset
5. Explain correlation is not causation with an example
6. Demonstrate knowledge of how to find out if a variable may be confounding
7. Give an example where a variable is found not to be confounding
8. Recognize how Chi-square test and Cramer's value are used together
9. Find the association between two categorical variables in terms of both: significance and strength
10. Use `histplot(discrete=True)` to plot proportions of categroical variables
11. Calculate the contingency table and plot the heatmap

Lessons:

- [Lab: Bivariate Analysis](./M3/lessons/04_bivariate.ipynb)

#### Exercises

1. [Hypothesis Testing](./M4/exercises/hypothesis_ex.ipynb) (4 questions)


## Projects

All three projects are required, but only one will be selected to present:

- [Project 1: Fast Food Marketing Campaign A/B Test](./Project/p1/p1_fast_food.qmd)
- [Project 2: Student Exam Performance Factors](./Project/p2/p2_student_exam.qmd)
- [Project 3: Global Mental Health Crisis Index 2026](./Project/p3/p3_GMHCI-2026.qmd)

See [Project Notebook Presentation](./Project/presentation.qmd).

## References

- https://openintro-ims.netlify.app/
- https://www.coursera.org/professional-certificates/google-advanced-data-analytics
- https://www.kaggle.com/learn/data-visualization
